seo_description: Current time in Tripoli, Libya. Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) year-round — no DST since 2013. Live clock and time differences for Tripoli.

faq.question: What time is it in Tripoli right now?
faq.answer: Tripoli, Libya uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) year-round. Libya does not observe Daylight Saving Time since 2013. The IANA identifier is Africa/Tripoli. Tripoli shares the UTC+2 offset with Cairo (Egypt), Athens (Greece), and Johannesburg (South Africa). The live clock above shows the current Tripoli time.

faq.question: What time zone is Tripoli in?
faq.answer: Tripoli, Libya is in the Eastern European Time zone (EET, UTC+2) year-round. The IANA identifier is Africa/Tripoli. Libya abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2013 — it briefly moved to UTC+1 (CET) between 2012 and 2013 but returned to UTC+2 permanently. Tripoli shares this offset with Cairo, Athens, Bucharest, and Johannesburg.

faq.question: Does Tripoli observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Tripoli and Libya do not observe Daylight Saving Time. Libya uses EET (UTC+2) permanently year-round since 2013. Prior to 2013, Libya briefly experimented with UTC+1 (2012–2013) but reverted to UTC+2 permanently.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tripoli and London?
faq.answer: Tripoli (EET, UTC+2) is 2 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1), Tripoli is 1 hour ahead of London. Libya does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tripoli and New York?
faq.answer: Tripoli (EET, UTC+2) is 7 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Tripoli is 6 hours ahead. Libya does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Tripoli?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Tripoli are 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM EET, Sunday through Thursday (Friday and Saturday are the weekend in Libya). Tripoli is Libya's capital and largest city, located on the Mediterranean coast. Libya has significant oil reserves — it holds Africa's largest proven oil reserves. The Central Bank of Libya and Libyan Investment Authority (LIA) sovereign wealth fund are based in Tripoli.
